criteria.1: Depicts exactly two silver metal balls with identical size, shape, and surface appearance at the start of the video
criteria.2: Depicts one ball visibly hotter (e.g., glowing red) and the other visibly cooler (e.g., not glowing) before contact
criteria.3: Depicts the two balls being placed in direct physical contact with each other
criteria.4: Avoids introducing any additional objects, entities, or actions not described in the prompt at any point in the video
criteria.5: Maintains the identity and properties of both balls throughout the video without unexplained or unprompted changes

criteria.1: Depicts the hot ball retaining its initial color and temperature until the moment of contact
criteria.2: Depicts the cool ball retaining its initial color and temperature until the moment of contact
criteria.3: Depicts color and temperature changes in both balls beginning only after contact is established
criteria.4: Depicts both balls reaching their final, identical appearance only after sufficient time has elapsed following contact
criteria.5: Avoids depicting any event (such as color change or heat transfer) occurring before its causative action (contact) has taken place

criteria.1: Depicts heat transfer occurring exclusively during direct physical contact between the balls
criteria.2: Depicts both balls reaching the same final color and temperature, consistent with thermal equilibrium
criteria.3: Depicts the hot ball cooling and the cool ball heating at rates consistent with energy conservation and identical material properties
criteria.4: Avoids depicting any temperature or color change in either ball prior to contact
criteria.5: Avoids depicting any spontaneous, unexplained, or non-physical changes in temperature or color at any time

criteria.1: Depicts heat flowing from the hot ball to the cool ball only after contact is made
criteria.2: Depicts both balls reaching the same final color and temperature, visually reinforcing the concept of thermal equilibrium
criteria.3: Depicts the cessation of temperature and color change in both balls once thermal equilibrium is reached
criteria.4: Avoids depicting or suggesting heat flow in the direction from the cool ball to the hot ball at any point

criteria.1: Depicts both balls changing color at the same rate after contact, consistent with identical material and mass
criteria.2: Depicts both balls reaching the same final appearance with no residual difference in color or glow
criteria.3: Avoids depicting any asymmetry in the color change process between the balls except for their initial temperature difference

criteria.1: Avoids depicting heat transfer or color change in either ball without direct physical contact
criteria.2: Avoids introducing visual effects or cues (such as sparks, flashes, or unexplained glows) not related to the described heat transfer process
criteria.3: Avoids depicting any external influence (such as a third object or environmental change) affecting the balls during the process
criteria.4: Avoids including ambiguous or confusing visual elements that could mislead viewers about the direction or mechanism of heat transfer
